(20 November 1987)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a [Welsh](/wiki/Wales "Wales") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") player for [London Welsh RFC](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC"). A [centre](/wiki/Centre_(rugby_union) "Centre (rugby union)"), he previously played for [Ebbw Vale](/wiki/Ebbw_Vale_RFC "Ebbw Vale RFC"), the [Newport Gwent Dragons](/wiki/Dragons_(rugby_union) "Dragons (rugby union)") and [Coventry](/wiki/Coventry_RFC "Coventry RFC"). In 2010 he had a trial with [Sale Sharks](/wiki/Sale_Sharks "Sale Sharks").

[Newport Gwent Dragons profile](http://www.newportgwentdragons.com/Personnel.aspx?pr=190206) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20090428195445/http://www.newportgwentdragons.com/Personnel.aspx?pr=190206) 2009-04-28 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [James Lewis (rugby player)](/w/index.php?title=James_Lewis_(rugby_player)&redirect=no "James Lewis (rugby player)"))

Welsh rugby union footballer

Rugby player

James Lewis

| Height | 1.88 m (6 ft 2 in) |
| Weight | 93 kg (14 st 9 lb) |
| Rugby union career | |

**James Lewis** (20 November 1987)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a [Welsh](/wiki/Wales "Wales") [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") player for [London Welsh RFC](/wiki/London_Welsh_RFC "London Welsh RFC"). A [centre](/wiki/Centre_(rugby_union) "Centre (rugby union)"), he previously played for [Ebbw Vale](/wiki/Ebbw_Vale_RFC "Ebbw Vale RFC"), the [Newport Gwent Dragons](/wiki/Dragons_(rugby_union) "Dragons (rugby union)") and [Coventry](/wiki/Coventry_RFC "Coventry RFC"). In 2010 he had a trial with [Sale Sharks](/wiki/Sale_Sharks "Sale Sharks").

The twin brother of [scrum-half](/wiki/Scrum-half_(rugby_union) "Scrum-half (rugby union)") [Robert Lewis](/wiki/Robert_Lewis_(rugby_player) "Robert Lewis (rugby player)"), James Lewis has represented Wales at U16, U18, U19, [U20](/wiki/Wales_national_under-20_rugby_union_team "Wales national under-20 rugby union team") and at [Sevens](/wiki/Wales_national_rugby_sevens_team "Wales national rugby sevens team"). He scored an outstanding [try](/wiki/Try_(rugby) "Try (rugby)") in Wales U19's win over France U19's in the 2005-06 [Grand Slam](/wiki/Grand_Slam_(rugby_union) "Grand Slam (rugby union)") decider – and played a part in the newly created team's [Six Nations Under 20s Championship](/wiki/Six_Nations_Under_20s_Championship "Six Nations Under 20s Championship") in 2007. He has represented Wales on 18 occasions across all the age grade international teams.

Lewis was selected for the senior Wales Sevens squad at 19 years of age for the tour to the [Hong Kong Sevens](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Sevens "Hong Kong Sevens") and the [Adelaide Sevens](/wiki/Adelaide_Sevens "Adelaide Sevens") for legs five and six of the [2006–07 IRB Sevens World Series](/wiki/2006%E2%80%9307_IRB_Sevens_World_Series "2006–07 IRB Sevens World Series"). He scored a try in the Plate quarter-final clash against [Italy](/wiki/Italy_national_rugby_sevens_team "Italy national rugby sevens team") in [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong"); his team ultimately won the trophy by overcoming [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina_national_rugby_sevens_team "Argentina national rugby sevens team") in the final.

He was the only player to represent Wales in all of the [2007–08 IRB Sevens World Series](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_IRB_Sevens_World_Series "2007–08 IRB Sevens World Series"), and the three [Rugby World Cup Sevens](/wiki/Rugby_World_Cup_Sevens "Rugby World Cup Sevens") qualifying games. He scored a try in the Bowl semi-final in the [Dubai Sevens](/wiki/Dubai_Sevens "Dubai Sevens") against [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_sevens_team "Australia national rugby sevens team") (which Wales narrowly lost), and further impressed in the [George Sevens](/wiki/South_Africa_Sevens "South Africa Sevens"); he notched a match-winning try against [France](/wiki/France_national_rugby_sevens_team "France national rugby sevens team") in the Bowl semi-final to put his side through to the final against [England](/wiki/England_national_rugby_sevens_team "England national rugby sevens team"), in which he also scored to gain Wales Sevens' first piece of silverware in the 2007–08 season.

In 2007-08 he was Wales and Europe's highest try scorer in the [IRB World Sevens Series](/wiki/IRB_World_Sevens_Series "IRB World Sevens Series") and the 13th highest try scorer in the World 7's with 20 tries. Lewis has accumulated 34 tries for the Wales Sevens in all tournaments.

He was one of only 5 players (his twin brother Robert was another) who were part of the London Welsh squads that won the Greene King IPA Championship in 2011-2012 and again in 2013-2014 and he played throughout both the club's seasons in the Aviva Premiership. In season 2015 - 2016 he scored a hat-trick of tries to help London Welsh beat Yorkshire Carnegie 33 - 10 at Headingley in April to win the British & Irish Cup and was awarded Man of the Match. In the final game of the season against Doncaster he made his 100th appearance for the club.

Following the unfortunate financial situation at London Welsh and its loss of a licence to continue to play in the RFU Greene King Championship, James returned to Ebbw Vale RFC in January 2017.

In 2023 James was unsuccessful in his attempt to become a Gladiator on BBC's new series of Gladiators. Speaking to fans after hearing the news he claimed to be committed to winning a place on the show. It was rumored he will perform under the name "Dragon".
